extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won from reduced group sprint after EVENEPOEL Remco's 106km solo attempt was caught, ACKERMANN Pascal lost red jersey cracking at 40km.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL launched 106km solo break despite being only 10s down overall, forcing constant pace-making from favorites.
ACKERMANN distanced at 40km reveals pure sprinters struggle on Deutschland Tour's cumulative terrain despite flat finales.
Bonus climb inside 18km forced selection before sprint, creating reduced group finish favoring classics-capable fastmen.
UAE Emirates committed to KRISTOFF despite chasing EVENEPOEL, shows willingness to defend while simultaneously protecting sprint option.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: attacked 106km out from 69th overall, forced sustained chase despite 10s deficit showing tactical fearlessness.
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: lost red jersey dropping at 40km on Deutschland Tour terrain, lacks endurance for cumulative climbing.
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: won from reduced group after closing gap, confirms ability to survive selections and still sprint.
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: neutral: British champion positioned well but beaten by KRISTOFF, confirms form present but lacks winning margin.
